[CT0540] Social network analysis

Ca' Foscari University of Venice
Prof. Fabiana Zollo

Application: Data Filtering and Text Analysis

Import Facebook Data

posts = read.csv("data/nyt_posts.csv")

dim(posts)

[1] 7481   36

names(posts)[1:23]

 [1] "Page.Name"              "User.Name"              "Facebook.Id"           
 [4] "Page.Category"          "Page.Admin.Top.Country" "Page.Description"      
 [7] "Page.Created"           "Likes.at.Posting"       "Followers.at.Posting"  
[10] "Post.Created"           "Post.Created.Date"      "Post.Created.Time"     
[13] "Type"                   "Total.Interactions"     "Likes"                 
[16] "Comments"               "Shares"                 "Love"                  
[19] "Wow"                    "Haha"                   "Sad"                   
[22] "Angry"                  "Care"

Import Facebook Data

names(posts)[24:36]

 [1] "Video.Share.Status"             "Is.Video.Owner."               
 [3] "Post.Views"                     "Total.Views"                   
 [5] "Total.Views.For.All.Crossposts" "Video.Length"                  
 [7] "URL"                            "Message"                       
 [9] "Link"                           "Final.Link"                    
[11] "Image.Text"                     "Link.Text"                     
[13] "Description"

Data Processing

p = posts[posts$Likes > 4 & posts$Comments > 4 & posts$Shares >4,]
dim(p)

[1] 7309   36

paste0((round(dim(p)[1]/dim(posts)[1],2))*100, "%")

[1] "98%"

Data Processing

head(p$URL)

[1] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793316504999"
[2] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793288534999"
[3] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793279864999"
[4] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793261429999"
[5] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793248829999"
[6] "https://www.facebook.com/5281959998/posts/10152793242129999"

library(stringr)
p$post_id =str_sub(p$URL, 43, 60)
head(p$post_id)

[1] "10152793316504999" "10152793288534999" "10152793279864999"
[4] "10152793261429999" "10152793248829999" "10152793242129999"

Data Processing

table(p$Type)


        Link Native Video        Photo       Status        Video 
        6499          307          496            6            1 

# Take posts of type Link
p_link = p[p$Type=="Link",] #6,499
df_link = data.frame(postid = p_link$post_id, link=p_link$Link)

Data Processing

# Take all other posts
p_other = p[!(p$post_id %in% df_link$postid),] #810

# Select only posts with a message
df_other = data.frame(postid = p_other$post_id, text=p_other$Message)
df_other = df_other[!(df_other$text==""),] #810

Data Processing

# How many posts have we selected? 
((nrow(df_link) + nrow(df_other)) / nrow(p) * 100) #100%

[1] 100

Data Filtering

# Other posts with message
m = as.character(df_other$text)
y = which(unlist(lapply(m, function(x){grepl("COVID-19", x, ignore.case=T)})))
posts_covid_m = df_other[y,]
str(posts_covid_m)

'data.frame':   25 obs. of  2 variables:
 $ postid: chr  "10152792313969999" "10152788996094999" "10152788919439999" "10152788887314999" ...
 $ text  : chr  "“The Covid-19 pandemic is not over, and the risk of both flu and Covid-19 circulating could put additional stra"| __truncated__ "700,000 people in the U.S. have now died of Covid-19 — making this the deadliest pandemic in American history. "| __truncated__ "Merck's antiviral pill could provide an effective way to treat Covid-19, health officials said on Friday, but t"| __truncated__ "Nearly 700,000 people in the U.S. have now died of Covid-19 — making this the deadliest pandemic in American hi"| __truncated__ ...

Data Filtering

# Posts of type Link
library(rvest)
library(data.table)

posts_covid_l = data.frame(postid=as.character(), link=as.character(), text=as.character())
links = df_link

Data Filtering

n = nrow(links)

for(i in 1:n){
  message(i)
  id = links[i,]$postid
  theurl = links[i,]$link 
  temp = data.frame(postid = id, link = theurl, text = "")

  simple = try(read_html(theurl), silent=T)

  if(!(class(simple) == "try-error")){
    text = simple %>% html_nodes("p") %>% html_text()
    text = str_c(text, collapse = " ")

    if(grepl("COVID-19", text, ignore.case=T)){
        message("Selected")
        temp$text = text
        posts_covid_l = rbind(posts_covid_l, temp) 
    }
  }
}

Data Filtering

str(posts_covid_l)

'data.frame':   1425 obs. of  3 variables:
 $ postid: chr  "10152793288534999" "10152793121859999" "10152793069834999" "10152793063589999" ...
 $ link  : chr  "https://nyti.ms/3DlTxXi" "https://nyti.ms/3AjGsMg" "https://nyti.ms/3lmucX5" "https://nyti.ms/3BpPgBn" ...
 $ text  : chr  " The French actress has roles in “No Time to Die,” “The French Dispatch” and the upcoming “France.” She sees ac"| __truncated__ " Fans are coming up with creative ideas to incorporate masks into their cosplay as in-person gatherings resume,"| __truncated__ "Advertisement By Adeel Hassan The sheriff of Los Angeles County reiterated this week that he would not compel m"| __truncated__ "Advertisement By Johnny Diaz The political battle in Florida over masks in schools escalated this week, as the "| __truncated__ ...

Data Filtering

postids_covid = c(posts_covid_m$postid, posts_covid_l$postid)
posts_covid_lm = merge(posts_covid_l, posts_covid_m, all=T)
str(posts_covid_lm)

'data.frame':   1450 obs. of  3 variables:

 $ text  : chr  "Haitian officials took the extraordinary step of requesting that the United States send in troops to protect th"| __truncated__ "Haitian officials took the extraordinary step of requesting that the United States send in troops to protect th"| __truncated__ "Advertisement Supported by The expansion of emergency reserves to help fund vaccines and pay down debt is polit"| __truncated__ "Advertisement Supported by Scientists were critical of the announcement, pointing to evidence that the current "| __truncated__ ...
 $ link  : chr  "https://nyti.ms/3r05HAk" "https://nyti.ms/3qXJT8g" "https://nyti.ms/3hqZoCn" "https://nyti.ms/3xw08M7" ...

Corpus Creation

library(quanteda)
source("stopwords_extended.R")

corpus = corpus(posts_covid_lm, text_field = "text")
summary(corpus)

Tokenization

doc.tokens = tokens(corpus)
doc.tokens = tokens(doc.tokens, remove_punct = TRUE, remove_numbers = TRUE)
doc.tokens = tokens_select(doc.tokens, stopwords(language = "en", source = "snowball", simplify = TRUE), selection ='remove')
doc.tokens = tokens_select(doc.tokens, stopmore, selection ='remove')
doc.tokens = tokens_tolower(doc.tokens)

N-grams

toks_ngram = tokens_ngrams(doc.tokens, n = 2)
toks_ngram

Document-feature matrix (DFM)

dfmat1 = dfm(toks_ngram) %>% dfm_trim(min_termfreq = 10)

Word Frequency

library(quanteda.textstats)
features_dfm = textstat_frequency(dfmat1, n = 100)
features_dfm$feature = with(features_dfm, reorder(feature, -frequency))

Word Frequency

library(ggplot2)
png("frequency.png", width=2000, height=1000, res=200)
ggplot(features_dfm, aes(x = feature, y = frequency)) +
  geom_point() + theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))
dev.off()

Word Frequency

library(quanteda.textplots)
textplot_wordcloud(dfmat1)

Text Analysis

# The kwic function (keywords-in-context) performs a search for a word and allows us to view the contexts in which it occurs
set.seed(10)
s = sample(tokens(doc.tokens), 20) #take a sample

png("lexical_dispersion.png", width = 2000, height = 1000, res=200)
kwic(s, pattern = "school") %>%textplot_xray()
dev.off()

Text Analysis

Topic Modelling

  • The Structural Topic Model (STM) allows us to incorporate metadata into our model and uncover how different documents might talk about the same underlying topic using different word choices
  • Users provide a corpus of N documents and a number K of topics
  • The most important user input in parametric topic models is the number of topics
  • There is no right answer to the appropriate number of topics

Topic Modelling

  • More topics will give more fine-grained representations of the data at the potential cost of being less precisely estimated
  • For short corpora focused on very specific subject matter 3-10 topics is a useful starting range
  • For small corpora (a few hundred to a few thousand) 5-50 topics is a good place to start
  • Application specific, but previous applications in political science with medium sized corpora (10k to 100k documents) have found 60-100 topics to work well
  • For larger corpora 100 topics is a useful default size

Topic Modelling

library(stm)
topic.count = 10
dfm2stm = convert(dfmat1, to = "stm")
model.stm = stm(dfm2stm$documents, dfm2stm$vocab, K = topic.count, data = dfm2stm$meta, init.type = "Spectral")

Topic Modelling

  • Each word in a document comes from exactly one topic
  • Each document is represented by the proportion of its words that come from each of the K topics
  • These proportions are found in the N by K theta matrix
  • Each of the K topics are represented as distributions over words
  • The K-by-V (number of words in the vocabulary) matrix logbeta contains the natural log of the probability of seeing each word conditional on the topic

Topic Modelling

png("topic-modelling.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res=200)
plot(model.stm, type = "summary", text.cex = 0.5)
dev.off()

Topic Modelling

Sentiment Analysis

For sentiment analysis, see also the Syuzhet Package

Sentiment Analysis

library('syuzhet')
  • Package syuzhet uses NRC Emotion lexicon
  • The NRC emotion lexicon is a list of words and their associations with eight emotions (anger, fear, anticipation, trust, surprise, sadness, joy, and disgust) and two sentiments (negative and positive)

Sentiment Analysis

sentiment = get_nrc_sentiment(posts_covid_lm$text)
td = data.frame(t(sentiment))
td[,1:5]

              X1  X2 X3 X4  X5
anger         65  65 12  7  51
anticipation  54  54 19 15  87
disgust       28  28  4  6  29
fear          84  84 15 19  92
joy           30  30 15  4  48
sadness       55  55 12 11  73
surprise      19  19  9  3  38
trust        114 114 27 25 111
negative     121 121 24 24 137
positive     140 140 57 39 169

Transformation and Cleaning

# Compute column sums across rows for each level of a grouping variable
td = data.frame(rowSums(td[-1]))
td

             rowSums.td..1..
anger                  26819
anticipation           42060
disgust                15769
fear                   41783
joy                    25170
sadness                31885
surprise               17745
trust                  63966
negative               67066
positive              100212

Transformation and Cleaning

names(td)[1] <- "count"
td

              count
anger         26819
anticipation  42060
disgust       15769
fear          41783
joy           25170
sadness       31885
surprise      17745
trust         63966
negative      67066
positive     100212

Transformation and Cleaning

tdw <- cbind("sentiment" = rownames(td), td)
tdw

                sentiment  count
anger               anger  26819
anticipation anticipation  42060
disgust           disgust  15769
fear                 fear  41783
joy                   joy  25170
sadness           sadness  31885
surprise         surprise  17745
trust               trust  63966
negative         negative  67066
positive         positive 100212

Transformation and Cleaning

rownames(tdw) <- NULL
tdw

      sentiment  count
1         anger  26819
2  anticipation  42060
3       disgust  15769
4          fear  41783
5           joy  25170
6       sadness  31885
7      surprise  17745
8         trust  63966
9      negative  67066
10     positive 100212

Sentiment Analysis

td_em  = tdw[1:8, ] # emotions
td_pol = tdw[9:10, ] # polarity

Plot Emotions

require("ggplot2")
ggplot(td_em, aes(x = sentiment, y = count, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "emotion") +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1), legend.title = element_blank())

Plot Polarity

require("ggplot2")
ggplot(td_pol, aes(x = sentiment, y = count, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "polarity") +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1), legend.title = element_blank())
